title: [phase:critique] [family:glm-review] [provider_hint:glm]\nReview only these local changes for remaining auth tasks. Focus on bugs, security regressions, missing tests, and whether scope is fully completed. Files: /Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/rate-limiter.ts /Users/masa...
summary_text: Findings: 1. High: the new tenant guard silently grants access to the shared fallback tenant instead of denying unmapped users. In [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L325) and [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L1690), `resolveTenantId()` falls back to `'default'` when `getTenantContext()` returns `null`. But [tenant-isolation.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/tenant-isolation.ts#L71) treats “no tenant mapping” as a missing association, not an allowed tenant. Any active user missing a `tenant_users` row now gets scoped into the same default tenant and can read/write shared tenant data instead of getting a 403. That is a cross-tenant authorization regression, and there is no test covering the unmapped-user path. 2. Medium: refresh rotation is destructive on transient failure. In [jwt-auth.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts#L258), `consumeRefreshToken()` deletes the old token first, and [jwt-auth.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts#L319) only then does user-state lookup and new-token issuance. If lookup or `generateRefreshToken()` fails, the caller’s valid refresh token is already gone, turning a temporary DB problem into a forced logout. I don’t see a transaction/rollback path or a test for “refresh insert fails after consume”. 3. Medium: backend refresh failures that should be recoverable are surfaced as `401`, so the new client-side auth/service handling will misclassify outages as expired sessions. [jwt-auth.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts#L328) collapses inactive-user and lookup-failure cases into `null`, and [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L522) converts any `null` rotation into `401 Invalid refresh token`. The frontend in [happy-endpoint-client.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L91) treats `401` as `auth_expired` and `503` as `service_unavailable`, so a refresh-path backend failure will now drive the wrong UX and recovery path. There’s no test for refresh-endpoint failure classification. Scope/test status: `apps/happy-web` contract tests passed locally. I could not run the worker-side Vitest suite in `cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy` because Vite tries to write temp config artifacts into that repo and the sandbox only allows read access there.
